tools: one type of {@item artisan's tools|PHB} of your choice

default: {@item Leather armor|phb}, a {@item dagger|phb|daggers} and any {@filter simple weapons|items|source=phb|category=basic|type=simple weapon}
(a) a {@item component pouch|phb} or (b) an {@item arcane focus|phb}
(a) an {@item explorer's pack|phb} or (b) a {@item scholar's pack|phb}

entries.name: Warmage
entries: An elf recites the motions for the simplest spell she knows, repeating them until she knows they are perfect. With a single, precise stroke, she unleashes a wave of arcane energy, focused into a searing blue beam.

A tiefling waves her hands to conjure floating greatswords of humming blue energy that dance about in mesmerizing and deadly arcs. One blade takes on an aspect of fire, and the other lightning, as she begins to cleave through the goblinoid horde.
Waving a black-and-red checkered banner, a dragonborn king charges into the fore. At his sides are warmages of all houses, a chessboard of elite arcanists, poised to turn the tide of battle.
The undisputed masters of cantrips, warmages turn the most fundamental types of magic into a deadly and precise art.

entries.name: Cantrip Masters
entries: While other spellcasters aspire to grander and more complex spells, warmages refine and master the most fundamental magic: cantrips. Warmages wield their magic in the same way that a warrior uses a sword, bow, or axe— as weapons to be perfected, not as an unknowable force to be feared. In contrast to sorcerers and wizards, their magic is a trainable skill, one that can be mastered by almost anyone with discipline and aptitude. Therefore, warmages stand among the most fearsome of spellcasters, with only the most rudimentary of spells.

entries.name: Strategic Geniuses
entries: Warmages have a long and storied history as masterful strategists and tacticians, both in grand battles and personal skirmishes. If a warmage is outgunned or outmanned, they’ll try to outthink their foes, which works more often than not. Warmages demonstrate an efficient kind of pragmatism, owing perhaps to their ability to solve many problems with just a few magical tools.

entries.name: Aligned Free Agents
entries: While some warmages are fully self-taught, the majority join a house: a loose confederation of warmages who practice together and share secrets of the trade. Houses teach invaluable battlefield strategy, but each individual house also touts their own brands of tactics and styles of magical tricks. The House of Knights, for example, emphasizes armor and martial prowess, whereas the House of Kings emphasizes battlefield strategy.

By way of metaphor, houses that value honor and teamwork above all else brand themselves after games of strategy, whereas those who epitomize self-reliance name themselves for games of chance. This division in the warmage houses is representative of a schism that dates back to the earliest warmage masters. Legend has it that the first two masters separated when one decided to found a college. The other, out of spite, built a casino outside its grounds.

entries.name: Creating a Warmage
entries: Warmages come from all walks of life, so as you build yours, consider where you first learned the fundamentals of magic, which you would go on to perfect. You might have picked up cantrips from a fellow adventurer or in the early stages of wizarding training. You could even be self- taught. Choose an Arcane Initiation which best fits your character’s history with magic.

Moreover, consider how you feel about magic as a whole. Are you envious of other spellcasters, or are you confident you could best them regardless? Did you once pine to be a wizard, but found you could never muster enough true magic to form a spell slot? Did you choose warmagistry to hone your talents to their absolute peak, or did you choose it to wield power no warrior can equal?

Lastly, consider your prospective warmage House. Though you don’t choose one until reaching 3rd level, your aspirations might shape which house you favor the most. If you would like to dabble with greater arcane magic, the House of Bishops is the best fit. If the clashing of swords at the frontlines piques your interest, consider the House of Knights.

entries.name: Quickbuild
entries: You can make a Warmage quickly by following these suggestions. First, Intelligence should be your highest ability score, followed by Constitution. Make Strength your third highest if you plan to take the House of Knights. Then choose the {@spell chill touch}, {@spell force dart|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage}, {@spell force weapon|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage}, and {@spell phantom grapnel|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} cantrips, and the Adventurer Arcane Initiation. Pick any background that emphasizes your magical discipline.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: At 1st level, you begin to learn the simple, yet potent, brand of spellcasting for which warmages are known.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: You know four {@filter Warmage cantrips|spells|level=0|class=Warmage} of your choice from the Warmage spell list. You learn additional warmage cantrips of your choice at higher levels, as shown in the Cantrips Known column of the Warmage table
Additionally, when you gain a level in this class, you can choose one of the warmage cantrips you know and replace it with another warmage cantrip

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your warmage spells, since you learn your spells through practice and mental discipline. You use your Intelligence whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In adition, you use your Intelligence modifier when setting the saving throw DC for a warmage spell when you cast and when making an attack roll with one.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use an {@item arcane focus|phb} as a spellcasting focus for your warmage spells

entries.name: Cantrip Spell Level
entries: Due to their focus on cantrips, warmages are extremely adept at punching through magical defenses. When you cast a warmage cantrip, it counts as a spell of a level equal to half your warmage level (to a maximum of 9th level) for the purposes of {@spell counterspell}, {@spell dispel magic}, and traits such as a {@creature rakshasa|MM|rakshasa’s} Limited Magic Immunity.

classFeature.name: Arcane Initiation
entries: Warmages come from all backgrounds and walks of life. At 1st level, choose where you first learned the basics of magic. The cantrips offered by your initiation don’t count against your number of warmage cantrips known.

entries.name: Making Your Own Initiation
entries: You can work with your GM to create an Arcane Initiation to suit your character’s background. Choose two spells of your choice from any class’s spell list; one cantrip should be suitable for combat, and the other for utility. Avoid choosing cantrips that are otherwise exclusive to another spellcaster

classFeature.name: Arcane Fighting Style
entries: Warmages learn that magic is the purest of weapons, and can be wielded just as easily as any other. At 1st level, choose one of the following fighting styles

classFeature.name: Warmage Edge
entries: Starting at 2nd level, once on each of your turns when you deal damage with a warmage cantrip, you can improve one damage roll of the spell, adding your Intelligence modifier to the roll if you don't already add it.

Starting at 5th level, and as you gain levels in this class, you also add extra dice to the damage roll for your warmage cantrips, as shown in the Cantrip Bonus Dice column of the Warmage table. For example, when you are at 5th level, you can improve the damage of the {@spell fire bolt} cantrip to deal fire damage equal to {@dice 3d10} + your Intelligence modifier on a hit.

classFeature.name: Warmage Trick
entries: Beginning at 2nd level, you learn to use {@optfeature CW:T|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|Warmage Tricks} , special techniques that alter the way you fight, move, and cast your spells. You learn two tricks of your choice for which you meet the prerequisites. At higher levels, you learn additional tricks as shown in the Tricks Known column of the Warmage table.
Additionally, when you gain a level in this class, you can choose one of the tricks you know and replace it with another trick for which you meet the prerequisites.

classFeature.name: Warmage House
entries: Upon reaching 3rd level, you choose a Warmage House, which teaches you a suite of magical techniques. Your choice grants you features at 3rd level, and again at 7th, 10th, 15th, and 18th level.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can’t increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Arcane Surge
entries: Starting at 5th level, you learn to tap into a vast reservoir of magical power and unleash it upon your foes. Once on each of your turns, when you deal damage with a warmage cantrip, you can double the number of damage dice dealt by the spell. You can’t use this feature on a spell that has scored a critical hit.
Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest. Starting at 11th level, you can use this feature twice between rests.

classFeature.name: Tactical Insight
entries: At 6th level, you learn how to use ambient magical power to defend yourself from your foes’ magical attacks. You can add your Intelligence modifier to saving throws you make against spells and other magical effects that deal damage.

classFeature.name: Warmage House Feature
entries: At 7th level, you gain a feature granted by your Warmage House

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can’t increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Warmage House Feature
entries: At 10th level, you gain a feature granted by your Warmage House

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can’t increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Strategic Deflection
entries: Starting at 14th level, when a creature casts a spell that forces you to make a saving throw to avoid damage, you can use your reaction to attempt to redirect some of the spell’s energy to a new target. If you succeed on your saving throw against the spell, choose another creature you can see within the spell’s range or up to 30 feet away from you, whichever is closer, to make a saving throw against the spell, using your spell save DC. The chosen creature can be the original spellcaster. On a failed save, the creature suffers the effects of the spell as if you had cast the spell and it had been the original target or been within the area of the spell.
Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Warmage House Feature
entries: At 15th level, you gain a feature granted by your Warmage House

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can’t increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Warmage House Feature
entries: At 18th level, you gain a feature granted by your Warmage House

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can’t increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Master Warmage
entries: At 20th level, you reach the pinnacle of your warmage prowess. Whenever you cast a cantrip that deals 4 dice of damage to a target, it instead deals 5 dice of damage (excluding your cantrip bonus dice). If you cast a cantrip that makes 4 attacks, it instead makes 5 attacks.

subclassFeature.name: House of Bishops
entries: The House of Bishops adopts warmages with arcane potential, expanding on essential magic principles with traditional spellcasting. Such warmages might be easily confused for wizards or sorcerers, for they don the same garb and wield many of the same spells, but the warmage focus on perfection still holds precedence over garnering a vast array of spells.

subclassFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: When you choose this house at 3rd level, you gain the ability to cast spells more potent than cantrips.

entries.name: Spell Slots.
entries: The Bishops Spellcasting table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your spells of 1st level and higher. To cast one of these spells, you must expend a slot of the spell’s level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Spells known of 1st-Level and Higher.
entries: You know three 1st-level wizard spells of your choice, two of which you must choose from the conjuration and evocation spells on the wizard spell list.

The Spells Known column of the Bishops Spellcasting table shows when you learn more wizard spells of 1st level or higher. Each of these spells must be a conjuration or evocation spell of your choice, except for the spells learned at 8th, 14th, and 20th level, and must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

Whenever you gain a level in this class, you can replace one of the wizard spells you know with another spell of your choice from the wizard spell list. The new spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots, and it must be a conjuration or evocation spell, unless you’re replacing the spell you gained at 3rd, 8th or 14th level.

entries.name: Warmage Edge.
entries: You can add additional damage with your warmage spells of 1st level or higher using your Warmage Edge feature, as if they were cantrips. You can’t add Warmage Edge damage to the magic missile spell.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Study
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in two of the following skills of your choice: {@skill Arcana}, {@skill History}, {@skill Investigation}, {@skill Medicine}, or {@skill Religion}.

subclassFeature.name: Mystical Companion
entries: At 7th level, you learn the {@spell find familiar} spell and can cast it as a ritual. The spell doesn’t count against your number of spells known.

When you cast the spell, you can choose one of the normal forms for your familiar or one of the following special forms: {@creature imp}, {@creature pseudodragon}, {@creature quasit}, or {@creature sprite}.

subclassFeature.name: Siege Casting
entries: At 10th level, your warmage spells deal double damage to objects and structures. Additionally, when you cast a warmage spell that requires you to make a spell attack roll, the spell’s range is doubled.

subclassFeature.name: Spell Sculpting
entries: Starting at 15th level, when you cast a warmage spell that affects other creatures that you can see, you can choose a number of them up to your Intelligence modifier. The chosen creatures automatically succeed on their saving throws against the spell, and they take no damage if they would normally take half damage on a successful save.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Dominance
entries: At 18th level, you learn a powerful magical trick. As a bonus action on your turn, you can expend a number of spell slots with a combined level of 6 or more to regain an expended use of your Arcane Surge.

subclassFeature.name: House of Cards
entries: Warmages who join the House of Cards approach combat the same way they do games of chance: with high stakes and calculated gambles. Each warmage of this house wields a special deck of cards tied to the very threads of fate, through which they can empower their magic with deft flurries of hands. However, while these warmages command incredible power, their magical surges are at the whims of the cards, and so they must make strategic, ingenious plays to master them.

subclassFeature.name: Bluff
entries: When you choose this house at 3rd level, your poker face carries you through practically any lie. You gain proficiency in the {@skill Deception} skill and with playing card sets, if you don’t already have it.
Additionally, you can use your Intelligence modifier, instead of Charisma, for Charisma ({@skill Deception}) checks.

subclassFeature.name: Deck of Fate
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you gain a deck of magical playing or tarot cards, called a Deck of Fate, through which you can enhance the power of your cantrips. Shuffle a standard 52-card playing card deck and draw a hand of five cards when you roll initiative.

Whenever you target a hostile creature with a warmage spell that deals damage, you can use your bonus action to play one or more cards from your hand to enhance the power of the spell. If the cards exactly match one of the results on the Hands table on the following page, the spell deals extra damage to one of its targets of your choice or grants you one additional effect, according to the result. If the cards don’t match a result, they have no effect. Aces count as ones for results. Once you play a card, place it on the bottom of your deck and draw until you have five cards in your hand again.

entries.caption: Optional Caption
rows: Any five sequential cards, regardless of house.
Any five cards of the same suit, regardless of order.
Any three of the same card.
Any two of the same card.
You gain temporary hit points equal to your Intelligence modifier.
Your movement speed increases by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.
You gain a +1 bonus to your AC until the start of your next turn.
You gain a +1 bonus to saving throws until the start of your next.

subclassFeature.name: High Stakes
entries: At 7th level, you’ve mastered a very risky card trick using your {@subclassFeature Deck of Fate|Warmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|Cards|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|3|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage}. On your turn, you can choose any number of cards from your hand and place them on the bottom of your deck (no action required), losing 2 hit points per card you chose. You then draw until you have five cards in your hand again.

subclassFeature.name: Dealer’s Choice
entries: At 10th level, you can use your bonus action to gain one of the following effects, chosen by the GM:

items: You gain resistance to all damage until the end of your next turn.
You teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 60 feet of you
You vanish into a safe, endless demiplane. At the start of your next turn, you reappear in the space you left or in the nearest unoccupied space if that space is occupied

entries: Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Card Reading
entries: Beginning at 15th level, once on each of your turns when you draw a card from your deck as part of your Deck of Fate feature, you can declare a suit (Clubs, Diamonds, Hearts, or Spades) and check the top card of your deck. If you guessed the suit correctly, you can take the Dash, Disengage, or Use an Object action as part of the bonus action used for the feature.

subclassFeature.name: Ace in the Hole
entries: By 18th level, you have become a deckmaster. Whenever you would draw one or more cards from your deck, draw twice as many. Put half of the cards drawn on the bottom of your deck and the other cards in your hand.

subclassFeature.name: House of Darts
entries: Every warmage understands the importance of precision, but the House of Darts flaunts a competitive excellence when it comes to landing shots. Daggers, bolts of magic, and of course, darts are their weapons of choice, but they don’t stop at mere bullseyes. A wire array of trick shots set these warmages apart from their contemporaries, even if they lack the grand strategies or discipline of the more martial colleges.

subclassFeature.name: Mystical Daggers
entries: When you choose this house at 3rd level, you learn the {@spell magic daggers|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} cantrip, which doesn’t count against your number of cantrips known.

subclassFeature.name: Trick Shot
entries: At 3rd level, you can curve your projectiles in mid-air to hit tricky targets. Whenever you make a ranged attack roll with a warmage cantrip or a thrown weapon, you can add or subtract a d4 from the d20 roll (to a maximum of 20 or a minimum of 1). Increasing the d20 roll to a 20 doesn’t score a critical hit. You can choose to use this ability after making the attack roll, potentially causing the attack to hit.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Bullseye
entries: Also at 3rd level, you can sink a shot exactly where you need to. Record the number rolled on the d20 whenever you make a ranged spell attack roll with a warmage cantrip. Rolls that have advantage and disadvantage record the higher and lower d20 roll, respectively. If a d20 roll is increased or decreased using your Trick Shot feature, use the new number. A recorded roll lasts for 1 minute.
If you roll one of your recorded numbers again when making a spell attack roll with a warmage cantrip, you gain one of the following benefits of your choice:

items: You can reroll the {@dice d20} and must use the new roll. Record this new {@dice d20} roll.
You can deal an extra {@damage 1d10} force damage to the target.
You regain an expended use of your {@subclassFeature Trick Shot|Warmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|Darts|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|3|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} feature.

subclassFeature.name: Flurry of Blades
entries: Beginning at 7th level, when you cast a cantrip that allows you to make multiple spell attacks, such as {@spell force weapon|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} or {@spell magic daggers|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage}, you can use your bonus action to make one additional spell attack with that cantrip.

subclassFeature.name: Intercepting Shot
entries: Starting at 10th level, as a reaction when an attacker you can see within 60 feet makes an attack against you or a friendly creature you can see within 30 feet of you, you can make a ranged spell attack using {@spell magic daggers|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} to interrupt the attack. If the result of your roll is greater than the result of the attacker, you can reduce the attack roll targeting you by 5 (to a minimum result of 1).

subclassFeature.name: Vanishing Toss
entries: At 15th level, you master the house’s signature technique for recovering darts. Whenever you cast the {@spell magic daggers|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} cantrip, you can replace one of your spell attacks with a special Vanishing Toss that embeds a {@spell magic daggers|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} in an object you can see within range. You can use your bonus action to teleport to the dagger as long as you are within 500 feet of it.
The dagger disappears after 10 minutes or if you use this ability to throw another Vanishing Toss.

subclassFeature.name: Dart Burst
entries: Beginning at 18th level, if you have recorded ten or more unique numbers using your {@subclassFeature Bullseye|Warmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|Darts|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|3|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} feature, you can use your action to clear all of your recorded numbers and throw a burst of darts. Each creature in a 60-foot cone must make a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes 1d8 force damage for each unique recorded number on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Once you use this feature, you can’t do so again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: House of Dice
entries: The House of Dice throws out the warmage convention of meticulous, deliberate spellcasting in favor of wild, chaotic magic that presses the odds. Everything from their magical bursts to their enchanted sets of dice can fail horribly, so it’s auspicious that they often keep their fingers on the scales of fate. These warmages have mastered not only a repertoire of cantrips, but also techniques to manipulate fortune itself, allowing them to risk everything, and more than often come out on top.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: Starting when you choose this house at 3rd level, your light fingers and unscrupulous tactics help you win the day. You gain proficiency in the {@skill Sleight of Hand} skill and dice sets, if you don’t already have it.

subclassFeature.name: Dice of Fate
entries: At 3rd level, you gain four Dice of Fate, which are d6s. Whenever you make an attack roll, ability check, saving throw, or damage roll, you can expend one Die of Fate and add it to the roll. You can wait until after you make the roll before deciding to expend the Die of Fate, but must decide before the GM determines the roll’s outcome. Once you expend a Die of Fate, it goes to the GM, who can use it to add it to a roll made by an NPC or monster. Once the GM has used a die, it passes back to you, and so on. When you finish a long rest, you regain all of your expended Dice of Fate, whether or not the GM has used them.
Beginning at 7th level, you have an additional two Dice of Fate, and you can add two Dice of Fate to your damage rolls with warmage cantrips

subclassFeature.name: Chaos Roll
entries: Also at 3rd level, you can expend two of your {@subclassFeature Dice of Fate|Warmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|Dice|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|3|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} as an action, rolling them on the table below to create a chaotic surge of energy.

rows: You cast {@spell fireball}, centered on yourself
Your AC is reduced by 2 until the start of your next turn
Each creature other than yourself within 60 feet of you can speak only in a babbling nonsense language, and can’t perform the verbal components of spells until the start of your next turn.
A 5-foot-radius sphere of butterflies, insects, or doves fills a location you choose within 60 feet of you, heavily obscuring the area until the start of your next turn.

You gain 7 temporary hit points, and keep the {@subclassFeature Dice of Fate|Warmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|Dice|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|3|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} instead of giving them to the GM.
You become {@condition invisible} until the end of your next turn, as per the {@spell invisibility} spell.
An object within 60 feet of you chosen by the GM explodes, dealing no damage to you or your allies, and dealing {@damage 3d6} fire damage to one creature caught in the blast.

You teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. Each creature within 5 feet of the destination must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC or take {@damage 2d6} force damage.
Choose a creature you can see within 60 feet of you. That creature takes {@damage 4d6} necrotic damage, and you regain hit points equal to the necrotic damage dealt.

You cast {@spell lightning bolt} and can add the {@subclassFeature Dice of Fate|Warmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|Dice|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|3|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} to the damage roll

subclassFeature.name: Loaded Dice
entries: By 7th level, you can subtly cheat your dice. Once on each of your turns when you roll a {@dice d6}, you can flip the die upside down. Note that on a balanced {@dice d6}, the top and bottom numbers add up to 7, so you can determine the bottom number by subtracting the top number from 7.

subclassFeature.name: Twisted Fate
entries: Starting at 10th level, the winds of chance follow your die rolls, rather than vice versa. When you make an attack roll or ability check with disadvantage on your turn, you can attempt to invert fate as a bonus action. Expend a {@subclassFeature Dice of Fate|Warmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|Dice|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|3|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|Die of Fate} and roll it. On a 5, you ignore disadvantage on the roll. On a 6, you instead have advantage on the roll.

subclassFeature.name: Roll the Bones
entries: Beginning at 15th level, you can channel the chaotic energy of your dice in an instant. As a reaction when you take damage from a creature you can see, you can expend two {@subclassFeature Dice of Fate|Warmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|Dice|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|3|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} to make a Chaos Roll.

subclassFeature.name: Steal Luck
entries: Starting at 18th level, when you roll initiative, roll a {@dice d6}. You steal that many {@subclassFeature Dice of Fate|Warmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|Dice|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|3|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} back from the GM

subclassFeature.name: House of Go
entries: The renowned House of Go predates the other warmage colleges by centuries. Unlike its successors, the strategies pioneered by the House of Go focus upon formations and cooperation, rather than individual excellence. Which isn’t to suggest that warmages of this college are anything other than brilliant strategists—they rival the House of Kings in this regard. Rather, the footsoldiers of the House of Go are greater than the sum of their parts, fluidly adopting motions to isolate and flank their foes.

subclassFeature.name: Formations
entries: Starting when you choose this house at 3rd level, you learn special tactics called Formations. You can use a bonus action to activate one of the following Formations, which lasts until you and your allies are no longer in that formation, you are incapacitated, die, or you use this feature to activate a different formation.

entries.name: Flank
entries: You can use this formation when you and an ally that isn’t incapacitated are adjacent to a hostile creature and on opposite sides of it. You can determine this by tracing a 5-foot-wide line from your space. If this line passes through the center of all three spaces, the hostile creature is flanked. You and your ally have advantage on attack rolls against the flanked creature.

entries.name: Solo
entries: You can use this formation when you have no allies within 5 feet of you. While in this formation, if you target a creature with a cantrip, that creature can’t make opportunity attacks against you for the rest of the turn or until you are no longer in this formation, whether you deal damage with the cantrip or not.

entries.name: Wall
entries: You can use this formation when you have no allies within 5 feet of you. While in this formation, if you target a creature with a cantrip, that creature can’t make opportunity attacks against you for the rest of the turn or until you are no longer in this formation, whether you deal damage with the cantrip or not.

entries.name: Flanking Rules
entries: If your table already uses optional flanking rules, using the Flanking formation gains an additional benefit: you and your ally also have advantage on saving throws you make against effects caused by the flanked creature.

subclassFeature.name: Opening Moves
entries: Starting at 7th level, you can spend 10 minutes planning an encounter in exact detail. When you do so, you can share your plan with up to six allies within 30 feet of you who can hear or see you and who can understand you. Each ally that understands the plan can gain advantage on an attack roll or ability check it makes within the next 10 minutes. If an ally rolls initiative during this time, it can’t use this feature to gain advantage after the end of the first round of combat.
Once you use this feature to make a plan, you can’t do so again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Hardened Formations
entries: Beginning at 10th level, while you are in a formation, any critical hit against you or an ally in your formation becomes a normal hit.

subclassFeature.name: Chain of Liberties
entries: By 15th level, your formation moves as if your allies were links in a chain. Whenever you move on your turn, you can spend movement to allow willing allies in your formation to move to locations you choose. You must spend movement separately for each ally you wish to move.

subclassFeature.name: End Game
entries: By 18th level, you can play out the last few moves of the game in rapid succession. On your turn, when you reduce a creature to 0 hit points with a warmage cantrip, you can cast another cantrip as a bonus action. You can apply Warmage Edge to this cantrip, even if you have already used it on your turn.

subclassFeature.name: House of Kings
entries: Warmages who train in the House of Kings specialize in tactics and strategy, learning age-old maneuvers to gain an edge in combat. Natural-born leaders, Kings work best with a cadre of supporters to perform their tactics and overwhelm the enemy. Moreso than all other warmages, those in the House of Kings treat life and death as a game of strategy to be understood and conquered.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: When you choose this house at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with medium armor, as well as battleaxes, lances, longswords, tridents, and warhammers

subclassFeature.name: Battle Tactics
entries: At 3rd level, you learn stratagems that are fueled by special dice called battle dice.

entries.name: Battle Dice.
entries: You have two battle dice, which are d8s. A battle die is expended when you use it. You regain all of your expended battle dice when you finish a short or long rest, or when you roll initiative.
Your battle die changes and more battle dice become available when you reach certain levels in this class, as shown on the table above.

entries.name: Using Battle Dice.
entries: Once per turn, you can expend a battle die to perform a stratagem of your choice.

entries.name: Saving Throws.
entries: When a stratagem calls for a saving throw to resist its effects, the saving throw DC is equal to your spell save DC.

subclassFeature.name: Stratagem: Blitz
entries: As a bonus action, when you hit a creature with an attack, you can expend one battle die to maneuver one of your comrades into a more advantageous position. Choose a friendly creature within 60 feet that can see or hear you. That creature can use its reaction to move up to half its speed without provoking opportunity attacks from the target of your attack.

subclassFeature.name: Stratagem: Check
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack, you can expend one battle die as a bonus action to force that creature to flee. The target must make a Charisma saving throw. A creature that is immune to being frightened automatically succeeds on this saving throw. On a failed save, the target must immediately use its reaction, if available, to move up to half its speed directly away from you.

subclassFeature.name: Stratagem: Flash of Brilliance
entries: When you make an Intelligence or Wisdom check, you can expend one battle die to add it to the check. You can choose to use this stratagem after the ability check is rolled but must decide before the GM says whether the check succeeds or fails.

subclassFeature.name: Stratagem: Gambit
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack, you can expend one battle die to give your allies an opening. The next creature other than you to make an attack against the target adds the battle die to their attack roll.

subclassFeature.name: Stratagem: Mystic Counsel
entries: You can use a bonus action and expend one battle die to give counsel to a creature that can hear you within 30 feet of you. Once in the next minute when the creature makes a saving throw against a spell it can choose to roll the battle die and add the result to the saving throw.

subclassFeature.name: Stratagem: Stalemate
entries: When you hit a creature with an attack, you can expend one battle die as a bonus action to hold that creature in place. Until the end of its next turn, the target can’t willingly move unless it first takes the Disengage action.

subclassFeature.name: Lead from the Front
entries: Starting at 7th level, you and each friendly creature within 120 feet of you that can see you ignore nonmagical difficult terrain.

subclassFeature.name: Tactical Master
entries: Starting at 10th level, friendly creatures within 10 feet of you add your Intelligence modifier to their saving throws against spells and magical effects that deal damage.

subclassFeature.name: Stratagem: Checkmate
entries: At 15th level, you learn the following stratagem:

When you hit a creature with a weapon or spell attack, you can use your bonus action and expend a battle die to direct one of your companions to strike. When you do so, choose a friendly creature who can see or hear you that is within reach of the creature you hit. The friendly creature can immediately use its reaction to make one weapon attack or cast a cantrip that requires an attack roll, adding the battle die to the attack’s damage roll.

subclassFeature.name: Grandmaster
entries: At 18th level, when you roll initiative, choose a number of friendly creatures up to your Intelligence modifier within 60 feet of you that can see or hear you. Each of the chosen creatures gains a battle die. You don’t expend battle dice for this feature.

Once within the next 10 minutes, a chosen creature can expend the battle die when it makes an attack roll, ability check, saving throw, or damage roll, adding the battle die to the roll. The creature can wait until after it makes the roll before deciding to expend the battle die, but must decide before the GM determines the roll’s outcome. A creature can have only one battle die from this feature at a time.

subclassFeature.name: House of Knights
entries: The House of Knights has a long and storied tradition of melding swordplay with the warmage’s potent spellcasting. Unlike other warriors, however, Knights carry neither swords nor shields into combat, for they can forge both out of magical force at a moment’s notice.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: When you choose this house at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with medium armor and martial weapons.

subclassFeature.name: Force Breastplate
entries: At 3rd level, plates of magical force reinforce your armor. While you are wearing light or medium armor or are under the effects of {@spell mage armor}, you can add your Intelligence modifier, instead of Dexterity, to your Armor Class.

subclassFeature.name: Mystical Weapon
entries: Also at 3rd level, you learn the {@spell force weapon|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} cantrip, which doesn’t count against your number of cantrips known

Additionally, you possess a weapon made entirely of magical force. Whenever you would draw a weapon, you can summon this weapon to your empty hand and transform it into any simple or martial weapon of your choice. This weapon deals force damage on a hit instead of its normal damage type. Whenever the weapon leaves your hand, it vanishes into a pocket dimension until you summon it again.

subclassFeature.name: Flurry of Blades
entries: Beginning at 7th level, when you cast a cantrip that allows you to make multiple spell attacks, such as {@spell force weapon|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} or {@spell magic daggers|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage}, you can use your bonus action to make one additional spell attack with that cantrip.

subclassFeature.name: Knight’s Ward
entries: Starting 10th level, you learn to forge a hardened magical barrier between you and your foes. As a bonus action, you can gain a number of temporary hit points equal to twice your warmage level, which last for 1 minute.
Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Tactical Maneuver
entries: At 15th level, you can spend your entire movement to teleport up to half your speed to an unoccupied space you can see.

subclassFeature.name: Field of Blades
entries: Starting at 18th level, you can summon a whirlwind of mystical weapons for a flurry of strikes. As an action on your turn, you can make a melee spell attack against up to five creatures you can see within 30 feet of you, with a separate attack roll for each target. On a hit, a target takes force damage equal to 2d10 + your Intelligence modifier.

subclassFeature.name: House of Lancers
entries: Warmages who join the House of Lancers are trained in fluid martial arts and meditative asceticism, melding the stoic discipline of monks with potent spellcasting. As such, they are unarmed elemental warriors, self-reliant and capable of feats unmatched by their peers. However, Lancers are notoriously neutral and abstain from the politics of the other warmage houses.
Lancers derive their name from an ancient precursor to the pawn, a piece remembered only in early variants of chess. It is a relic from a forgotten age, rife with symbolism.

subclassFeature.name: Intercept Technique
entries: Starting when you choose this house at 3rd level, you adopt the monastic principle of offensive protection. While you aren’t wearing armor, your AC equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Intelligence modifier. If you are under the effects of the mage armor spell, your AC equals 13 + your Dexterity modifier or your Intelligence modifier (your choice). You can’t use a shield and gain the benefit of this feature.

subclassFeature.name: Hand-to-Hand Arcana
entries: Also at 3rd level, you learn the Lancers’ secret unarmed combat technique, granting you the following benefits:

items: You can use Intelligence instead of Strength for the attack and damage rolls of your unarmed strikes.
You can roll a {@dice d6} in place of the normal damage of your unarmed strikes.
Your unarmed strikes count as melee weapons for the purposes of warmage spells.
When you use the {@action Attack} action with an unarmed strike on your turn, you can make one unarmed strike as a bonus action.

entries.name: Converter Note
entries: Hi, ElMurlocElegante here! This homebrew is a conversion of the 1.1 version of the Complete Warmage (2014), by Mage Hand Press. Since then, a newer version for 2024 D&D has been released, which includes fixes for some features and changes to others. I, personally, recommend applying the following changes, which I extracted from that version:
{@s You can roll a {@dice d6} in place of the normal damage of your unarmed strikes.}
You can roll a {@dice d6} in place of the normal damage of your unarmed strikes. The damage die increases when you reach 5th level ({@dice d8}), 11th level ({@dice d10}) and 17th level ({@dice d12}).
{@s When you use the {@action Attack} action with an unarmed strike on your turn, you can make one unarmed strike as a bonus action.}
You can make an unarmed strike as a bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Shock Trooper
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you can rapidly close the distance between you and your foes. Whenever you make a melee attack on your turn against a creature you can see, you can lunge up to 15 feet toward your target before making the attack. Doing so doesn’t spend movement or provoke opportunity attacks. You can perform this movement even if it causes you to travel through the air, though you fall after making the attack if you have nothing holding you aloft.

subclassFeature.name: Mystical Physicality
entries: Starting at 7th level, whenever you make a Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution check, you can use your Intelligence modifier instead of the normal modifier.
Additionally, difficult terrain doesn’t cost you extra movement.

subclassFeature.name: Deflect Energy
entries: By 10th level, you can deflect bolts of energy with your bare hands. As a reaction when you are hit by a ranged spell attack or a ranged weapon attack that deals cold, fire, force, lightning, necrotic, or radiant damage, you can use your reaction to deflect the bolt. The damage you take from the attack is reduced by 1d10 + your Intelligence modifier + half your warmage level (rounded down).

subclassFeature.name: Improved Shock Trooper
entries: Starting at 15th level, you can lunge up to 30 feet using your {@subclassFeature Shock Trooper|Warmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|Lancers|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|3|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} feature. This movement causes you to teleport through creatures and objects, blinking to the target in an instant. You can’t teleport into an occupied space.

subclassFeature.name: Flurry of Spells
entries: Starting at 18th level, you can cast spells with superhuman speed. As an action, you can expend a use of your {@classFeature Arcane Feature|Warmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|5|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} feature to cast three different cantrips that have a casting time of 1 action or 1 bonus action. You can’t use your {@classFeature Arcane Feature|Warmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|5|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} feature on any of these cantrips.
Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: House of Pawns
entries: By far the most prominent warmage house is that of the Pawns. Warmages who join the House of Pawns learn to stretch their skill with cantrips to its limits. Pawns can master any cantrip or trick known to the other warmage houses, for they embody the adaptability to which all warmages aspire.

subclassFeature.name: Promotion
entries: When you choose this house at 3rd level, you gain the adaptability of the quintessential warmage. You learn one warmage trick of your choice, which doesn’t count against your number of warmage tricks known. Additionally, whenever you learn a warmage trick, you can choose a trick that has the House of Bishops, House of Kings, House of Knights, or House of Rooks as a prerequisite, as long as you meet all of the trick’s other prerequisites.

subclassFeature.name: Adaptive Arcanist
entries: Also at 3rd level, you learn to emulate the spellcasting prowess of other warmages. When you finish a short or long rest, choose one warmage cantrip. You learn this cantrip, which doesn’t count against your number of cantrips known, until you choose a different one with this feature.

subclassFeature.name: Pawn Storm
entries: Beginning at 7th level, if you target a creature with a cantrip, that creature can’t make opportunity attacks against you for the rest of the turn, whether you deal damage with the cantrip or not.

subclassFeature.name: Additional Arcane Fighting Style
entries: At 10th level, you can choose a second option from the Arcane Fighting Style class feature. You can’t take the same Arcane Fighting Style option more than once.

subclassFeature.name: Opening Move
entries: Starting at 15th level, you can add your Intelligence modifier to your initiative rolls. Additionally, when you roll initiative and you aren’t surprised, you can move up to your speed.

subclassFeature.name: Fundamental Mastery
entries: Starting at 18th level, your magic always finds its way to your foes’ most vulnerable spots. Once per turn when you roll damage for a warmage cantrip, you can choose one damage die and treat it as having rolled its maximum value.

subclassFeature.name: House of Rooks
entries: The clandestine members of the House of Rooks make adept spies, assassins, and scouts, though they might just as easily pick up jobs run by rogues and brigands. In addition to their suite of cantrips, Rooks are light on their feet and know a few tricks to magically open doors in utter silence.

subclassFeature.name: Rook Strike
entries: Starting when you choose this house at 3rd level, as a bonus action, you can gain advantage on the next attack roll you make on your turn. Alternatively, you can impose disadvantage on a saving throw a creature makes against a warmage spell you cast before the end of your turn
Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest, or until you reduce a creature to 0 hit points with a warnage cantrip.

subclassFeature.name: Rasp
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you can cast the knock spell at will without expending a spell slot. When you cast the spell in this way, it has a casting time of 1 minute and the spell’s casting is completely silent.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Acrobat
entries: Beginning at 7th level, you can add your Intelligence modifier to all Dexterity checks you make. Additionally, when you fall and aren’t incapacitated, you can subtract up to 60 feet from the fall when calculating falling damage.

subclassFeature.name: Fleeting Decoy
entries: Beginning at 10th level, as a reaction when you take damage from a creature you can see, you raise a defensive illusion to protect you from further harm. Attacks made against you have disadvantage until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Elusive Step
entries: By 15th level, you’re extremely difficult to pin down. If you move more than 15 feet on your turn, any subsequent movement you make doesn’t provoke opportunity attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Flash of Feathers
entries: Starting at 18th level, you can cast the {@spell invisibility} spell once, targeting only yourself, without expending a spell slot or spell components. While you are invisible, your speed is doubled, and you can make one attack or cast one warmage cantrip without ending the spell.
Once you use this feature to cast {@spell invisibility}, you can’t do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: House of Roulette
entries: Like the Houses of Dice and Cards, warmages of the House of Roulette are masters of the calculated gamble. Bearing a number of magical chips, they can push the odds when the need arises, pulling off longshot plays that other warmages could only dream of.

subclassFeature.name: Spin the Wheel
entries: Starting when you choose this house at 3rd level, you gain a roulette ball infused with arcane energy and a number of chips. As a bonus action, you can conjure a roulette wheel of magical force and roll the ball. Choose one of the bets from the Roulette Bets table below, announce your guess, and roll a d100. You gain the listed benefit if your guess is correct.

entries.caption: Roulette Bets
rows: {@b Odd/Even.} Guess whether the roll is even or odd.
You gain a +2 bonus to your AC until the start of your next turn.
{@b High/Low.} Guess whether the roll is above or below 50.
You gain a +2 bonus on attack rolls you make until the start of your next turn.
{@b Lanes.} Guess which of the following spans the number falls within: 01–20, 21–40, 41–60 , 61–80, 81–00.
You can gain advantage on an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw you make before the end of your next turn.
{@b Tens.}Guess the 10s digit of the roll.
Once before the end of your next turn, you can add 10 to the damage roll of a warmage spell you cast.
{@b Single.} Guess the exact number of the roll.
Until the start of your next turn, you have resistance to all damage and treat any {@dice d20} roll you make as 20.

subclassFeature.name: Chips of Fate
entries: You have four Chips of Fate, and regain all expended chips when you finish a long rest. You gain two additional Chips of Fate at 7th level (6 chips total), two more at 10th level (8 chips total), and two more at 15th level (10 chips total).

Whenever you place a bet on the roulette wheel, you can expend one to four of your chips to place additional bets equal to the number of chips expended. No matter how many chips you expend, you can only place two types of bets, such as Singles and Tens, per d100 roll. You can’t place more than one Odd/Even or High/Low bets. You can gain two benefits at once if you win two bets on the same roll.

subclassFeature.name: Lucky Number
entries: Beginning at 7th level, you can thumb the scales of fortune in favor of a longshot bet. Pick a Lucky Number from 1 to 100. If you roll that number on any {@dice d100} roll of your {@subclassFeature Spin the Wheel|Warmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|Roulette|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|3|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} feature, you gain the benefit of betting that number as a Single. You can change your Lucky Number whenever you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Spinning Shield
entries: Starting at 10th level, as a reaction when a creature you can see makes an attack roll against you, you can conjure your roulette wheel between yourself and the attacker. You and the attacker each roll a {@dice d100}. If your roll is equal or higher, the attack misses, and you can’t use this ability again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Cash Out
entries: By 15th level, you can unleash the energy stored within a Chip of Fate to escape a tight spot. As a bonus action, you can expend a Chip of Fate to teleport up to 120 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

subclassFeature.name: Everything on Black
entries: Starting at 18th level, you regain 4 expended Chips of Fate whenever you roll initiative. Additionally, whenever you expend one or more Chips of Fate to bet on a Single and win, you regain all of your expended Chips of Fate.

optionalfeature.name: Adventurer
entries: You picked up your magic informally by traveling with a dozen different mages over the years. You learn the {@spell mage hand} and {@spell ray of frost} cantrips.

optionalfeature.name: Circus Performer
entries: You learned a few simple tricks to participate in a sideshow or circus act. You learn the {@spell dancing lights} and {@spell minor illusion} cantrips.

optionalfeature.name: Eldritch Event
entries: An influx of insidious magic left an imprint on you. You learn the {@spell chill touch} and {@spell message} cantrips.

optionalfeature.name: Mercenary
entries: You mastered the fundamentals of war magic to engage in battle with similarly armed arcanists. You learn the {@spell arc blade} and {@spell true strike} cantrips.

optionalfeature.name: Temple
entries: A monastery or temple educated you in the ways of gentle healing magic. You learn the {@spell sacred flame} and {@spell spare the dying} cantrips.

optionalfeature.name: Tower Apprentice
entries: You apprenticed under a spellcaster for some time, who taught you the fundamentals of arcana. You learn the {@spell prestidigitation} and {@spell shocking grasp} cantrips.

optionalfeature.name: Self-Taught
entries: You taught yourself all the fundamentals of magic from a dusty old tome or abandoned scroll. You learn the {@spell fire bolt} and {@spell light} cantrips.

optionalfeature.name: Survival
entries: To survive in the wilderness, you taught yourself to cast simple spells. You learn the {@spell druidcraft} and {@spell shillelagh} cantrips.

optionalfeature.name: Blaster
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to the saving throw DCs of your warmage spells.

optionalfeature.name: Deflector
entries: When you have at least one hand free and a creature hits you with a spell attack or a ranged weapon attack, you can use your reaction to add your proficiency bonus to your Armor Class for that attack, potentially causing it to miss

optionalfeature.name: Resistive
entries: While you are wearing light armor or are under the effects of the {@spell mage armor} spell, you gain a +1 bonus to your Armor Class.

optionalfeature.name: Sniper
entries: When making a ranged spell attack, you gain a +1 bonus to the attack roll. Additionally, you ignore half cover when casting a warmage cantrip.

optionalfeature.name: Striker
entries: When you hit with a cantrip that requires a melee attack and exceed the target’s AC by 5 or more or score a critical hit, you can add your proficiency bonus to the damage roll.

optionalfeature.name: Blackjack

otherSummary.entrySummary: House of Cards, House of Darts, House of Dice, House of Roulette

entries: When you cast a cantrip that requires a single spell attack roll and you don’t have disadvantage on the roll, you can choose to roll with advantage. The attack automatically misses if the sum of the two rolled dice exceeds 21, and it is a critical hit if the sum equals 21.

optionalfeature.name: Blasting Cantrip
entries: Once on each of your turns, when you deal force damage to a creature with a warmage cantrip, you can push the creature up to 10 feet away from you in a straight line.

optionalfeature.name: Blinding Light
entries: When you use the {@spell light} cantrip to target an object you are holding, you can direct a flare at a creature you can see within 10 feet of you, which must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target is {@condition blinded} until the start of your next turn. After a creature has failed a saving throw against this trick, it has advantage on all saving throws against it for the next 24 hours.

optionalfeature.name: Chivalrous Presence
entries: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Insight} and {@skill Persuasion} skills, and you have advantage on ability checks you make to interact with nobility. Additionally, other creatures can always discern when you are telling the truth.

optionalfeature.name: Cloak of Feathers
entries: While you aren’t wearing armor or wielding a shield, your AC equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Intelligence modifier.

optionalfeature.name: Commander's Steed

otherSummary.entry: Prerequisites: {@subclass Go|Warmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|House of Go} or {@subclass Kings|Warmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|House of Kings}
otherSummary.entrySummary: House of Go or House of Kings

entries: You learn the {@spell find steed} spell and can cast it at will without expending a spell slot. Your steed is more resilient than most; its hit point maximum is increased by an amount equal to your warmage level.

optionalfeature.name: Contingent Cantrip
entries: When you finish a long rest, you can spend 10 minutes performing a ritual to create a contingent cantrip. Cast a warmage cantrip that has a casting time of 1 action or 1 bonus action and doesn’t deal damage. The cantrip doesn’t take effect immediately, but instead it takes effect the next time you speak a specified command word (no action required). When you perform this ritual, choose whether the cantrip targets you, the nearest friendly creature, or the nearest hostile creature. The cantrip can’t use other warmage tricks when it comes into effect.
You can only have one contingent cantrip at a time. If you perform this ritual again, the previous contingent cantrip ends.

optionalfeature.name: Corrosive Cantrip
entries: Once on each of your turns, when you deal acid damage to a creature with a warmage cantrip, you can cause the acid to erode the target’s defenses. The next time a creature makes an attack roll against the target before the start of your next turn, the target takes a penalty to its Armor Class equal to half your warmage level (up to a maximum of −5) for that attack.

optionalfeature.name: Draining Cantrip
entries: Whenever you deal necrotic or poison damage to a hostile creature with a warmage cantrip, you can siphon some of its life force. You gain temporary hit points equal to half your warmage level, which last for 1 minute.

optionalfeature.name: Encryptogram
entries: Your knowledge of ciphers has improved your magically clandestine communications. When you cast the {@spell cryptogram|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} cantrip, its limit is 20 characters, instead of 8, and only the specified recipient can read the message.

optionalfeature.name: Explosive Cantrip
entries: Once on each of your turns, when you deal fire damage to a creature with a warmage cantrip, you can cause it to explode. Each creature within 5 feet of the target, except you and the target, must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw against your spell save DC or take half the fire damage dealt to the target.

optionalfeature.name: Extended Range
entries: When you cast a warmage cantrip that has a range of 5 feet or greater, its range is doubled.

optionalfeature.name: Field Medic
entries: You learn the {@spell spare the dying} cantrip, which doesn’t count against your number of cantrips known. Additionally, when you cast {@spell spare the dying} on a creature that has 0 hit points, the target regains 1 hit point and gains temporary hit points equal to your warmage level, which last for 1 minute. Once a creature regains hit points from this trick, it can’t do so again until it finishes a long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Flexible Range
entries: Being within 5 feet of a hostile creature doesn’t impose disadvantage on your ranged spell attack rolls. Additionally, when you cast a warmage cantrip that requires a melee spell attack, its range increases to 10 feet.

optionalfeature.name: Gamble

otherSummary.entry: {@spell Cheat|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} cantrip, {@subclass Cards|Warmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|House of Cards}, {@subclass Darts|Warmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|House of Darts}, {@subclass Dice|Warmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|House of Dice}, or {@subclass Roulette|Warmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|House of Roulette}
otherSummary.entrySummary: House of Cards, House of Darts, House of Dice, or House of Roulette

entries: You are always under the effects of the {@spell cheat|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} cantrip. Additionally, when you miss with an attack roll, or you fail an ability check or aving throw, you can reroll the d20 and must use the new roll. Once you use this ability, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Infinite Variation
entries: You have become exceptionally skilled at using the {@spell prestidigitation} cantrip to mimic other spells. When you cast {@spell prestidigitation}, you can use it to emulate the effects of any other cantrip that doesn’t deal damage, even one that is not on the warmage spell list. To do so, you must succeed on a DC 15 Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}) check, otherwise the spell fails. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for a cantrip cast using this trick, and it counts as a warmage cantrip for you.

optionalfeature.name: Icy Cantrip
entries: Once on each of your turns, when you deal cold damage to a creature with a warmage cantrip, you can numb the target with a frigid blast. The first time the target makes an attack roll before the end of its next turn, subtract half your warmage level from the attack roll.

optionalfeature.name: Leading Edge Tactics
entries: You always have a plan when engaging the enemy. Attacks against you have disadvantage during the first round of combat. You can’t use this feature when you are surprised.

optionalfeature.name: Mage Hand Knack
entries: Your skill with the {@spell mage hand} cantrip allows you to use it as an extension of yourself. When you cast the spell and as a bonus action on your subsequent turns, you can use one of the following effects with the hand:

items: **Press.** The hand pushes against a Large or smaller creature within 5 feet of it. Choose a direction away from that creature. Every foot of movement in that direction while the hand is pressing against it costs the creature 1 extra foot of movement. The hand continues to push against the target until the spell ends or you use your bonus action to use a different effect with the hand.
**Punch.** The hand strikes one creature or object within 5 feet of it. Make a melee spell attack for the hand. On a hit, the target takes 1d6 force damage.

**Seize.** The hand grabs a Small or smaller creature and attempts to grapple it. The creature must succeed on a Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check (its choice) against your spell save DC or be grappled by the hand. The hand continues to grapple the target until the target uses an action to break the grapple on its turn, the spell ends, or you use your bonus action to use a different effect with the hand.

optionalfeature.name: Magic Moment
entries: When you cast {@spell moment to think|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage}, you can choose to also stop time for a willing or {@condition unconscious} creature you can see within 30 feet. The creature can take one of the actions permitted by the spell during your turn, and you can interact with one another while time is stopped. During this time you can exchange items, swap locations, take the {@action Help} action for tasks while time is stopped, attempt to stabilize a dying creature, or have a short conversation without eavesdropping or interruption.

entries.name: Converter Note
entries: Hi, ElMurlocElegante here! This homebrew is a conversion of the 1.1 version of the Complete Warmage (2014), by Mage Hand Press. Since then, a newer version for 2024 D&D has been released, which includes fixes for some features and changes to others.
In this newer version, it is implicit that you can only swap places with the willing creature if you are within 5 feet of one another

optionalfeature.name: Minor Shadow Illusion
entries: When you create the image of an object in an unoccupied space using the {@spell minor illusion} cantrip, you can fill it with fibers of shadowstuff, causing it to become partially real. No matter what form the semi-real object takes, it still must be no larger than a 5-foot cube. It has AC 10 and 5 hit points, and it weighs 5 pounds. You can only have one semi-real illusion at a time. While this semi-real object exists, the cantrip requires your concentration.

The illusion can’t replicate a creature, but it can deal damage to a creature within its 5-foot cube. If the illusion is of an object that can deal damage, a creature that enters the object’s 5-foot cube or begins its turn there must make an Intelligence saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature takes 1d6 damage of a type appropriate to the illusion. This damage increases by {@dice 1d6} when you reach 5th level ({@dice 2d6}), 11th level ({@dice 3d6}), and 17th level ({@dice 4d6}). This damage can’t trigger {@classFeature Warmage Edge|Warmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|2|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} or any warmage tricks.

optionalfeature.name: Mystical Armor
entries: You can cast the {@spell mage armor} spell on yourself at will, without using a spell slot or spell components.

optionalfeature.name: Mystical Athlete
entries: When you cast the {@spell quickstep|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} cantrip, your speed increases by 20 feet instead of 10 feet. When you cast the {@spell springheel|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} cantrip, your jumping distance increases by 20 feet instead of 10 feet. If you know both of these cantrips, you can cast both of them as part of the same bonus action.

optionalfeature.name: Mystical Retort
entries: You learn the {@spell vicious mockery} cantrip, which doesn’t count against your number of cantrips known.

Additionally, when a creature you can see makes an attack roll against you, you can cast {@spell vicious mockery} targeting that creature as a reaction. If the creature fails its saving throw, the cantrip imposes disadvantage on the triggering attack roll, instead of the creature’s next attack roll, potentially causing the attack to miss.
Once you cast this cantrip as a reaction, you can’t do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Mystical Weaponmaster
entries: Once on each of your turns when you roll a 1 on the d20 for an attack roll with the {@spell force weapon|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} or {@spell magic daggers|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} cantrips, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll.

optionalfeature.name: Mystical Vision
entries: You can cast the {@spell detect magic} spell at will without expending a spell slot.

optionalfeature.name: Phantom Hookshot
entries: You can cast the {@spell phantom grapnel} cantrip as a bonus action. If you do so, its range is reduced to 15 feet. When you cast the spell as an action, creatures are pulled an extra 10 feet toward you by the spell.

optionalfeature.name: Prismatic Cantrip
entries: Once on each of your turns, when you deal force damage with a warmage cantrip, you can roll a {@dice d6} on the following table to change the cantrip’s damage type for this casting. You can apply tricks to the casting as normal using the new damage type.
Additionally, you can apply the trick corresponding to the rolled damage type, even if you don’t know the trick. You can do so twice and regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Radiant Cantrip
entries: Once on each of your turns, when you cast a spell that deals acid, cold, fire, force, lightning, poison, or thunder, you can change the damage type to radiant. You can reroll one of the cantrip’s damage dice and must use the new roll.
The number of damage dice you can reroll increases by 1 when you reach 11th level (2 dice) and 17th level (3 dice).

optionalfeature.name: Rapid Fortification
entries: You can cast the {@spell mending} cantrip as an action or a bonus action. If you cast it as an action, you can use one of the following effects:

items: You can restore a single nonmagical object, such as a door, cart, or window, to pristine condition if at least half of its parts are present. This object can be no larger than 10 cubic feet, or 1 cubic foot if it is exceptionally complex (such as a clock).

You can create simple fortifications, such as sealing a door shut, adding wooden planks to a window, or building a short stone wall (no larger than 10 cubic feet). You must have the materials present to use this ability.

optionalfeature.name: Residual Cantrip
entries: When you cast a cantrip that deals damage and requires a single spell attack roll and you miss, you can channel some of the residual magic into a secondary beam of arcane energy as a bonus action. Make a ranged spell attack against a creature you can see within the cantrip’s range. On a hit, the target takes 1d6 damage of the cantrip’s damage type. This beam doesn’t count as a cantrip.
This damage increases to {@dice 1d8} when you reach 5th level, to {@dice 1d10} when you reach 11th level, and to {@dice 1d12} when you reach 17th level.

optionalfeature.name: Severe Cantrip
entries: When a creature rolls a 1 on a saving throw against one of your warmage cantrips, it automatically fails the save and takes twice the number of damage dice dealt by the spell, as if you scored a critical hit. The additional damage only applies to the creature that rolled a 1.

optionalfeature.name: Silent Cantrip
entries: Once on each of your turns when you deal thunder damage to a hostile creature with a warmage cantrip, you can create a 15-foot-diameter sphere of magical silence (as per the {@spell silence} spell), centered on yourself or the creature (your choice), which lasts until the start of your next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Static Cantrip
entries: Whenever you deal lightning damage to a hostile creature with a warmage cantrip, you can sap part of the energy into a charge, which clings to your body until the start of your next turn. While charged, you can use your reaction when you take damage from a creature you can see within 5 feet of you to deal lightning damage equal to half your warmage level to the creature.

optionalfeature.name: Signature Focus
entries: When you finish a long rest, you can place a unique sigil on a simple or martial weapon, which becomes your signature focus until you use this ability again. This weapon becomes magical, and you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your warmage spells. Your signature focus is bonded to you, and gains a number of special abilities:

items: As a bonus action, you can call your signature focus to your hand, as long as you are on the same plane of existence as it.
You can add your Intelligence modifier, instead of your Strength or Dexterity modifier, to attack and damage rolls using your signature focus.

Your signature focus gains a number of charges equal to your Intelligence modifier (a minimum of 1). When you deal damage to a creature with it or a cantrip cast through it, you can expend 1 charge to deal an extra {@dice 1d8} force damage to that creature. Your focus regains all expended charges when you finish a long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Split Fire
entries: When you cast a warmage cantrip that requires a single spell attack roll, you can select multiple creatures and make a spell attack roll against each one. You can target a number of creatures equal to the number of damage dice the cantrip deals, and split your damage dice up amongst your targets, to a minimum of 1 die of damage per target. Each attack must target a different creature.

For example, {@spell fire bolt} deals {@dice 3d10} damage. You can choose to target three creatures and deal {@dice 1d10} damage to each creature, or you can target two creatures, dealing {@dice 1d10} damage to one creature and {@dice 2d10} damage to the other creature, or you can target one creature for {@dice 3d10} damage.

optionalfeature.name: Bishop's Maneuver
entries: You can take the {@action Disengage} action as a bonus action. When you do so, your walking speed increases by 10 feet until the end of your turn.

optionalfeature.name: Capture
entries: You gain the following formation:

**Capture.** You can use this formation when you and three or more allies that aren’t incapacitated are adjacent to a hostile creature and surround it. You can determine this by placing an imaginary cube around the hostile creature. If you and your allies are adjacent to each side of the cube, the creature is surrounded.

When you activate this formation, the hostile creature must make a Dexterity saving throw against your warmage spell save DC or be enveloped by chains of magical force and {@condition stunned} for 1 minute. At the end of each of the creature’s turns, it can make a Dexterity saving throw, freeing itself and ending this formation on a success.
Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Castle

otherSummary.entry: Prerequisites: 10th level, {@subclass House of Rooks|Warmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|House of Rooks}

entries: As an action, you can choose a willing Small or Medium creature you can see within 120 feet of you. You both teleport, swapping places.
Once you use this trick, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Chips are Down
entries: When you expend one or more {@subclassFeature Chips of Fate|Warmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|Roulette|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|3|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} to bet and lose at least one bet, you regain one expended {@subclassFeature Chips of Fate|Warmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|Roulette|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|3|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage}.

optionalfeature.name: Dartmaster
entries: When you cast a cantrip that allows you to make multiple ranged spell attacks, such as {@spell magic daggers|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} or {@spell force dart|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} using the {{@optfeature Split Fire|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} trick, and you miss with one or more attacks, you can reroll all of your misses.
Once you use this trick, you can’t do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Directed Momentum
entries: Once on each of your turns, when you score a critical hit with a melee attack or reduce a creature to 0 hit points with one, you can make an unarmed strike against a second target. If the target is within range of your Shock Trooper feature, you can lunge toward it. On a hit, the target takes an extra 1d8 force damage.

optionalfeature.name: Fold
entries: You have learned to graciously accept defeat using your Deck of Fate. As a {@action reaction} when you are hit by an attack, you can play your entire hand to cast the {@spell shield} spell without expending a spell slot.
Once you use this trick, you can’t use it again until you finish a rest short or long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Gauntlet Upgrade
entries: You install a high-power module in your blaster gauntlet. Whenever you cast a warmage cantrip using your gauntlet as the spellcasting focus, you can use one of the following warmage tricks. You can’t use other warmage tricks when you use one of these tricks. Once you use one of these tricks, you can’t use that trick again until you finish a long rest.

items: **Beam.** When you cast a warmage cantrip that requires one or more spell attack rolls and deals damage, you can focus the cantrip into a 5-foot-wide, 60-foot-long line. Each creature in the line must make a Dexterity saving throw against your warmage spell save DC. On a failed save, a creature takes 2d10 damage for each point of your proficiency bonus. The damage type is the same type that the cantrip deals. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage. You can’t use your Warmage Edge or Arcane Surge features when you use this trick.
**Overheat.** The cantrip’s damage dice become d12s.

optionalfeature.name: Knight's Aegis
entries: When you cast the {@spell force buckler|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage} cantrip, you can concentrate on it for up to 1 minute. The spell doesn’t end early if you are hit by an attack.

optionalfeature.name: Lieutenant's Demand
entries: You can cast the spell {@spell command} at will without expending a spell slot.

optionalfeature.name: Snake Eyes
entries: If you roll a 1 or 2 on a {@subclassFeature Dice of Fate|Warmage|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|Dice|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|3|MageHandPressCompleteWarmage|Die of Fate}, you keep the die instead of giving it to the GM.

optionalfeature.name: Unerring Strike
entries: When you cast the {@spell true strike} cantrip, you can concentrate on it for a number of rounds equal to your Intelligence modifier. You gain advantage on the first attack roll you make against the target on each of your turns while maintaining concentration on {@spell true strike}.

spell.name: Card Trick
entries: With a flash of your hands, you fling a playing or tarot card charged with energy at your opponents. Choose whether you make a ranged spell attack roll or for the target to make a Dexterity saving throw. On a hit or a failed saving throw, the target takes {@damage 1d6} force damage.
This spell's damage increases by {@dice 1d6} when you reach
5th level ({@dice 2d6}), 11th level ({@dice 3d6}), and 17th level ({@dice 4d6}).

spell.name: Caustic Blade
entries: As part of the action used to cast this spell, you must make a melee attack with a weapon against one creature within the spell's range, otherwise the spell fails. On a hit, the target suffers the weapon attack's normal effects, except that any damage dealt by the attack is acid damage instead of its normal type. If you miss by 3 or less, acid splashes on the target, and you instead deal {@damage 1d8} acid damage.

This spell's damage increases when you reach certain levels. At 5th level, the melee attack deals an additional {@damage 1d8} acid damage to the target on a hit, and the acid damage dealt on a miss increases to {@dice 2d8}.
